title: The Sufferings of the Pleasure Syndrome.To play the media you will need to either update your browser to a recent version or update yourFlash plugin
justification: This talk provides the most comprehensive and coherent exploration of 'The Pleasure Syndrome' theme, covering the psychology of desire, the cycle of seeking happiness through sensual pleasure, the consequences of indulgence (boredom, frustration, grief, attachment), and the path of renunciation. It offers rich examples, personal anecdotes (Mother Teresa, crack addiction, TV programs), and maintains excellent flow while connecting practical meditation guidance with deeper Buddhist psychology. The talk has strong literary potential with its accessible voice and memorable illustrations.
summary: This dharma talk explores how seeking happiness through sensual pleasure creates a syndrome of desire, attachment, and suffering, examining the psychology behind our consumerist tendencies and emotional dependencies. Bhante offers practical guidance on renunciation as spiritual empowerment, using vivid examples to illustrate how letting go of desire leads to contentment and inner strength.

relevance.note: Directly addresses the pleasure syndrome and its sufferings
